rulings.Case Title: BRIAN HURLEY et al VS. GRANT RICHARDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Grant Richards, Scott Aal, Grant, And Scott & Hurley, Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 22, Defendants Grant Richards, Scott AAl, Grant, and Scott & Hurley, LLC'S Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative Summary Adjudiction. (continued from May 4, 2009, to May 5, 2009). Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is granted as to 10th cause of action for breach of implied contract, and 11th cause of action, breach of employment contract. Kusek declaration objections sustained under D'Amico vs. Board of Med Examiners (74) 11 Cal.3rd 1. Motion for summary adjudication is denied as to remaining causes of action. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN HURLEY et al VS. GRANT RICHARDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Brian Hurley And Kathleen Kusek'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 22, Plaintiffs Brian Hurley and Kathleen Kusek's Motion for Summary Adjudication (continued from May 4, 2009 to May 5, 2009). Motion is denied. Cross-complainants raise a triable issue of fact as to whether or not cross-defendants were conspiring to divert business opportunity to Firehouse from GS&H. A triable issue of fact is raised as to whether Hurley was intentionally misrepresenting the Torani account to cross-complainants so the business could be diverted to Firewood, causing GS&H to lose that business and therefore be damaged. =(302/CWW/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PATRICK TERRY VS. MARTINDALE WASHINGTON et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 1, Plaintiff Daniel Patrick Terry's Order to Show Cause regarding Contempt (per ex parte Order of 3/16/09). rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CHANDLER PROPERTIES'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Off Calendar. Insufficient notice. CCP section 437c. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CHANDLER PROPERTIES'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Chandler is the property manager, not the landlord. No violations of the Rent Ordinance are alleged against Chandler. Menefee does not apply. A two year statute of limitations applies against Chandler. The primary right theory does not apply. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MAY TSAI et al VS. CONNIE FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 5, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CONNIE FUNG, RONALD FUNG, and, FUNG TRUST'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Central to plaintiff's complaint is the construction of the garage and the resulting damages as alleged in the third amended complaint and in the Rent Board petitions. These claims are governed by CCP section 340(a) and the holding in Menefee vs. Ostawari (1991) 228 Cal.App.3rd 239. Plaintiffs have estblished through three unsucessful attempts to amend that they are unable to state a claim that is not barred by the statutute of limitations. =(302/CWW/JW)
